mnemonics: SODA: Similarity, Overlap, Distance, Asymmetry - The four main types of proximity measures
keyPoints: Proximity measures quantify how similar or dissimilar data points are
Distance metrics like Euclidean and Manhattan measure dissimilarity
Similarity metrics like Cosine and Jaccard measure how alike two points are
commonMistakes: Assuming all proximity measures are symmetric (some are not, like KL-Divergence)
Using Euclidean distance without normalizing features of different scales
Confusing distance (dissimilarity) with similarity (higher values mean more alike)
